stories.headline: Sara Duterte impeachment pretrial concludes; BIR evidence box remains sealed
stories.summary: The five-day pretrial conference for Vice President Sara Duterte's Senate impeachment trial concluded on June 25, with prosecution and defense teams having marked evidence and identified witnesses. A sealed Bureau of Internal Revenue box allegedly containing Duterte's tax records was not opened after the defense objected; prosecutors marked only the sealed box itself. The defense has until Friday to respond to outstanding prosecution requests, and the trial proper is scheduled to begin July 6.
stories.context: Duterte was impeached by the House of Representatives on charges including misuse of confidential funds, threats against the President and other officials, and unexplained wealth. The pretrial conference, the first phase before the trial proper, began June 18.
stories.watch_for: The Senate impeachment trial is scheduled to begin on July 6, 2026.

stories.headline: Ombudsman orders Senators Cayetano and Padilla to answer obstruction charges in Dela Rosa arrest standoff
stories.summary: The Office of the Ombudsman directed Senate Minority Leader Alan Peter Cayetano and Senator Robinhood Padilla, along with suspended Senate sergeant-at-arms Ma O. Aplasca, to respond within 15 days to obstruction of justice charges under Presidential Decree 1829. The complaint alleges they frustrated the enforcement of an arrest warrant by placing former Senator Ronald Dela Rosa under Senate custody, transporting him off the premises, and blocking NBI agents from entering.
stories.context: Dela Rosa, wanted on an International Criminal Court warrant, returned to the Senate in May 2026 after six months in hiding. NBI agents and Senate security personnel exchanged gunfire during the standoff.
stories.watch_for: Cayetano, Padilla, and Aplasca have 15 days from receipt of the order to file their counter-affidavits with the Ombudsman.

stories.headline: Official once punched by Sara Duterte named as prosecution witness for Senate impeachment trial
stories.summary: House prosecutors listed Abe Andres, a Davao City official who was punched four times by then-Mayor Sara Duterte during a 2011 demolition standoff, as a witness for the upcoming impeachment trial. According to the prosecution brief, Andres will testify under Article 4 of the impeachment complaint, which charges Duterte with culpable violation of the Constitution and betrayal of public trust. The testimony is intended to demonstrate the respondent's propensity for violence.
stories.context: The 2011 incident occurred when Duterte struck Andres after he refused to delay a court-ordered demolition of an informal settlement. The altercation was filmed and broadcast on television.
stories.watch_for: The Senate impeachment trial is scheduled to begin on July 6, 2026.

stories.headline: DepEd orders nationwide school safety audits and security upgrades after Tacloban shooting
stories.summary: Education Secretary Sonny Angara ordered nationwide school safety audits and directed the Philippine National Police to enhance security at schools following the June 22 shooting at San Jose National High School in Tacloban City. Angara convened officials from the PNP, NBI, and the Cybercrime Investigation and Coordinating Center to coordinate intelligence sharing and threat response. He announced funding for metal detectors and fence repairs at public schools and said psychological support has been deployed to affected students and staff.

stories.headline: Tacloban school shooting: two minor suspects killed three students, police say
stories.summary: Two minor suspects, aged 14 and 15, opened fire on students at San Jose National High School in Tacloban City on June 22, killing three and wounding 20 others. The 15-year-old was apprehended at the school; both were described by neighbors and teachers as quiet individuals with no prior history of troublemaking. President Marcos called the incident unacceptable and directed authorities to determine the cause; Interior Secretary Remulla announced plans to propose a law imposing criminal liability on gun owners whose licensed firearms are used by others to commit crimes.

stories.headline: Authorities take 14-year-old into custody in Leyte over social media threat to attack school
stories.summary: Interior Secretary Jonvic Remulla said authorities took a 14-year-old student into custody in Leyte on June 24 after she allegedly used multiple Facebook accounts to post threats of an attack at Tolosa National High School. A tip from Senator Bam Aquino prompted the response. The minor, who had no access to firearms, was turned over to the Department of Social Welfare and Development rather than detained, as she is below the minimum age of criminal liability.

stories.headline: Marikina instructor dies after motorcycle struck by SUV driven by vice mayor's son, who tested positive for alcohol
stories.summary: Alexander Quintos Cruz, a 27-year-old instructor at Pamantasan ng Lungsod ng Marikina, died from a brain hemorrhage after his motorcycle was struck by an SUV at an intersection in Barangay Concepcion Dos on June 13. The driver, identified as David Alfred de Guzman, son of Marikina City Vice Mayor Del de Guzman, tested positive for alcohol intoxication at the time of the collision. CCTV footage confirmed Cruz had activated his turn signal. Cruz's family filed his death certificate to seek an upgrade of charges against de Guzman to reckless imprudence resulting in homicide.
stories.context: De Guzman was initially charged with reckless imprudence resulting in physical injuries and drunk driving violations after the June 13 collision. Cruz spent about a week hospitalized before dying. The vice mayor allegedly contacted police during the initial investigation.

stories.headline: Severe Tropical Storm Gardo enters Philippine area as Francisco exits; no wind signals raised
stories.summary: PAGASA reported that Tropical Storm Gardo intensified into a Severe Tropical Storm on June 25 evening after entering the Philippine Area of Responsibility, with maximum sustained winds of 95 kph and gusts up to 115 kph, located 1,275 kilometers east of extreme northern Luzon. No tropical cyclone wind signals have been raised in any part of the country. Severe Tropical Storm Francisco, known internationally as Mekkhala, exited the Philippine Area of Responsibility earlier in the day.
stories.watch_for: PAGASA said Gardo may weaken to a tropical storm by Friday afternoon or evening and is expected to exit the PAR by Friday morning or afternoon.

stories.headline: Recto directs Cabinet departments to accelerate 2026 spending and finalize 2027 budget priorities
stories.summary: Executive Secretary Ralph Recto directed Cabinet departments including Health, Transportation, Education, Budget Management, and Social Welfare to accelerate their 2026 budget implementation and begin finalizing 2027 spending priorities. A review meeting assessed whether projects and services are on schedule and reviewed the UPLIFT assistance program for vulnerable sectors. The 2027 national budget must be submitted to Congress within 30 days after the State of the Nation Address.
stories.context: Economic analysts have cited government underspending as a factor in the Philippines' weaker growth outlook for 2026, alongside elevated global oil prices. The NEDA recently projected the economy could grow 3.5 to 4.5 percent this year, below the government's 5 to 6 percent target.
stories.watch_for: The 2027 national budget is due to Congress within 30 days of the July 26 State of the Nation Address.

stories.headline: BSP orders banks to adopt biometrics and behavioral analysis to fight cyber fraud beyond OTPs
stories.summary: The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas issued Circular No. 1213 directing financial institutions to adopt biometrics, behavioral analysis, adaptive verification, and passwordless authentication, moving beyond one-time passwords to counter increasingly sophisticated cyber fraud. The BSP said cybercriminals have evolved to use phishing, SIM-swapping, malware, and social engineering to bypass OTP protections. Banks must also implement proactive fraud detection systems to identify suspicious transactions and unusual behavior.

stories.headline: Multi-agency operation seizes 4.72 million packs of illicit cigarettes in Cebu, worth P343M in unpaid taxes
stories.summary: A multi-agency operation led by the Bureau of Internal Revenue's Central Visayas office seized approximately 4.72 million packs of illicit cigarettes across two sites in Cebu City: seven shipping containers in Barangay Bacayan and three near Pier 7. The BIR estimated the basic excise tax liability at P343.12 million. The operation also involved the NBI, PNP, Bureau of Customs, and Philippine Coast Guard; additional enforcement was ongoing at Pier 3.

stories.headline: Mayon Volcano records lava flows and rockfalls on 172nd consecutive day of eruption
stories.summary: The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology reported lava flows and pyroclastic density currents from Mayon Volcano in Albay on the evening of June 25, marking the 172nd consecutive day of the volcano's ongoing effusive eruption. Alert Level 3 remains in effect, and entry into the six-kilometer Permanent Danger Zone around the crater is prohibited.

stories.headline: Newly graduated student shot by unidentified gunmen outside college in Davao de Oro
stories.summary: Unidentified gunmen opened fire on a recently graduated student outside Kolehiyo ng Pantukan in Pantukan, Davao de Oro, on June 25 afternoon as he was preparing to board a vehicle with family members after a graduation ceremony. The victim was struck multiple times by a suspected .45 caliber firearm and is in stable condition at a provincial hospital. The suspects fled in a gray van without license plates; police have launched a pursuit operation.

stories.headline: Man drowns at artificial lake formed by June 8 earthquake landslide in Davao Occidental
stories.summary: A man drowned while swimming at an artificial lake formed by landslides triggered by the June 8 earthquake in Barangay San Isidro, Jose Abad Santos, Davao Occidental. The local mayor said the victim had been drinking near the lake with a relative before entering the water and did not resurface. The lake formed when the earthquake's landslides blocked a river, flooding nearby communities; authorities had opened a passage in the dam on June 24 to restore normal water flow.

stories.headline: Twin earthquakes kill at least 188 in Venezuela; La Guaira declared disaster zone
stories.summary: Two earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 struck Venezuela on the evening of June 24, killing at least 188 people and injuring approximately 1,520, with about 200 still trapped awaiting rescue, according to National Assembly President Jorge Rodriguez. La Guaira was declared a disaster zone after a hospital there was evacuated due to structural damage. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio pledged a prompt and effective response to the disaster.

stories.headline: Kenya police arrest 355 people as demonstrators mark two years since deadly 2024 protests
stories.summary: Kenyan police deployed water cannons, tear gas, and roadblocks across the country as demonstrators gathered to mark the second anniversary of 2024 youth-led protests against tax increases. Interior Minister Kipchumba Murkomen confirmed 355 arrests in various locations. Families of victims and activists continue to seek accountability for at least 60 people killed by security forces when protesters stormed Parliament in June 2024; President Ruto has allocated 2 billion Kenyan shillings for abuse victims, which activists say is insufficient.

stories.headline: IMO suspends Strait of Hormuz sailor evacuation after Singapore-flagged vessel attacked
stories.summary: The International Maritime Organisation suspended its operation to evacuate stranded sailors from the Strait of Hormuz after a Singapore-flagged vessel was attacked in the waterway; the IMO chief said the struck vessel had not been transiting under the UN evacuation framework. About 11,000 sailors had been awaiting evacuation. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio, meeting with Gulf Cooperation Council states in Bahrain, said the US would defend Gulf interests and denied that any reconstruction fund for Iran had been agreed upon.
stories.context: The Strait of Hormuz was disrupted after US and Israeli strikes on Iran in late February. A US-Iran memorandum of understanding signed on June 17 set a 60-day negotiation window, but maritime traffic through the strait remains below pre-war levels.
stories.watch_for: The June 17 memorandum set a 60-day window for negotiations on Iran's nuclear program and conditions for restoring normal shipping through the strait.

stories.headline: China's maritime patrols east of Taiwan draw formal objections from US, Europe and Taiwan
stories.summary: China conducted maritime law enforcement operations in waters east of Taiwan, framing them as a response to sea boundary discussions between Japan and the Philippines. Taiwan's government called the Japan-Philippines talks a pretext for the patrols and warned of new Chinese pressure in the region. The United States and Europe both issued formal objections to the operations, which have raised concerns about collision risks and escalating tensions.

stories.headline: Russia's Ukraine offensive stalls as Ukrainian drone strikes reach deep into Russian territory
stories.summary: Russia's summer offensive to expand its hold on southeastern Ukraine has stalled at minimal gains despite heavy casualties, with analysts attributing the slowdown partly to Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian supply lines and fuel infrastructure. Ukrainian drones struck Yekaterinburg in the Urals, shutting its airport repeatedly, while attacks on refineries have caused fuel shortages and economic disruption inside Russia. President Putin has signaled openness to peace talks based on 2022 Istanbul negotiations, terms Ukrainian officials are expected to reject.
